title: O.7c: Fix Qwen3 MoE lazy-KV memory growth — async_eval state after each decode step
description:
Problem: run_decode_step only evals outputs[0] (logits). All 20 KV/SSM arrays stay lazy. Each step's KV holds a reference to the previous step's KV, building a 3574-deep computation chain. MLX can't free intermediate buffers while the chain is alive. Observed: 20 GB → 32 GB on a 3574-token generation.

Fix: async_eval the KV and SSM output arrays immediately after each decode step, same pattern as LlamaModel moe_generate_pipelined. This breaks the lazy chain so each step's intermediate graph state is freed before the next begins.

Files: compute/src/compute/model/qwen3_moe_model_mlx.cpp — run_decode_step, moe_generate_pipelined

Reference: llama_model.cpp:1260-1279 (async_eval pattern)

title: O.7a: Batched prefill for Qwen3 MoE (T>1 SSM kernel + parallel attn)
description:
Why: prefill() currently loops T sequential decode steps — T separate Metal dispatches per SSM layer, and O(T²) attention with one KV-grow per token. mlx-lm does one pass over the full prompt. Speed gap is 12.2 vs 88.4 tok/s (7.25x).

What to do:
1. mlx_ssm_step → add mlx_ssm_prefill(x [1,T,hidden], T, conv_state, rec_state, ...) that:
   - runs conv1d over all T tokens at once
   - calls gated_delta_kernel() with T>1 (kernel already supports this)
   - grid: {32, Dv, T*Hv} — z-dim is B*Hv where B=1 still, T is loop inside kernel
   - returns y [1,T,Hv*Dv] and updated rec_state
   - NOTE: conv_state still only needs the last (ks-1) tokens as new state
2. mlx_full_attn_prefill(x [1,T,hidden], ...) that runs RoPE+SDPA over full T-token sequence
3. build_prefill_fn(): separate compiled function (mx::compile shapeless=true) that takes full prompt [1,T] token ids, runs all layers in one pass, returns logits[T] + updated KV/SSM state
4. prefill() updated to call build_prefill_fn() path instead of T×run_decode_step()
5. Kernel is already done (T-loop + b_idx support added in previous session)

Files: compute/src/compute/model/qwen3_moe_model_mlx.cpp, .h
notes: Implementation approach refined: NOT using build_prefill_fn() with mx::compile — the custom Metal kernel stores output_shapes_ at creation time with T baked in, so shapeless compile breaks when called with different T. Instead: run_prefill() runs eagerly (no compile). Wins still large: SSM goes from T dispatches to 1 dispatch per layer (T-loop inside kernel), attention goes from T sequential SDPAs to 1 SDPA per layer. MoE loops T times per layer (router is data-dependent). mlx_ssm_step and mlx_full_attn_step unified to handle [1,T,hidden] for both decode (T=1) and prefill (T>1). build_decode_fn() updated to use [1,1,hidden] shape to match unified API.
close_reason: Implemented. mlx_ssm_step and mlx_full_attn_step now handle [1,T,hidden] for both decode (T=1) and prefill (T>1). run_prefill() runs eagerly: 1 SSM Metal dispatch per layer, 1 SDPA per attn layer. build_decode_fn() updated for 3D hidden shape. Both integration tests pass.

title: O.7b: Remove full-model mx::compile, revert to growing KV
description:
Why: Full-model mx::compile is wrong granularity for Qwen3 MoE. mlx-lm confirmed: no full forward-pass compile for hybrid MoE models.

What to do:
1. In build_decode_fn(): change mx::compile(std::move(fn)) back to: mlx_state_->compiled_fn = std::move(fn)
2. Revert KV to growing-via-concat: remove chunk expansion logic from run_decode_step
3. In init_empty_decode_state(): revert to {nkv, 0, hd} empty KV
4. In mlx_full_attn_step(): remove kv_len = kv_k.shape()[1] and causal mask, revert to: auto new_k = mx::concatenate({kv_k, k_rope}, 1) with maskless SDPA over actual filled positions
5. Remove #include <mlx/compile.h>

File: compute/src/compute/model/qwen3_moe_model_mlx.cpp
close_reason: Wrong direction — output_shapes patch makes shapeless compile compatible with custom Metal kernel.

title: O.7a: Replace GatedDeltaNet custom Metal kernel with compiled pure MLX ops
description:
Why: gated_delta_kernel() custom Metal kernel blocks mx::compile fusion. mlx-lm uses pure MLX ops and wraps with mx::compile(shapeless=True).

What to do:
1. Remove gated_delta_kernel() call and kGatedDeltaDecodeSource constant
2. Replace with pure MLX ops in mlx_ssm_step():
   - decay = reshape(g_seq, {Hv,1,1})
   - state = state * decay
   - kv_mem = sum(state * expand_dims(k_3d,-2), -1)
   - delta = (v_3d - kv_mem) * expand_dims(beta_seq,-1)
   - state = state + expand_dims(k_3d,-2) * expand_dims(delta,-1)
   - y = sum(state * expand_dims(q_3d,-2), -1)
3. Extract into mlx_gated_delta_step() and wrap with mx::compile(shapeless=True)
4. Shapes in decode are always fixed: q/k {Hk,Dk}, v {Hv,Dv}, g/beta {Hv}, state {Hv,Dv,Dk}
5. Remove: mx::fast::TemplateArg usage, kGatedDeltaDecodeSource, gated_delta_kernel() helper

File: compute/src/compute/model/qwen3_moe_model_mlx.cpp
close_reason: Wrong direction — mlx-lm uses custom Metal kernel in inference. Replacing with mx::sum is a regression (189 MB/token intermediates).

title: O.7: Fix Qwen3 MoE speed gap (12.2 -> >=50 tok/s)
description:
Why: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit runs at 12.2 tok/s on M2 Ultra vs 88.4 tok/s in Python mlx-lm (7.25x gap). Phase O delivered the MLX-native model class but mx::compile is not properly applied.

Root cause (confirmed via mlx-lm investigation): The custom GatedDeltaNet Metal kernel in mlx_ssm_step() blocks mx::compile fusion. mlx-lm does NOT compile the full forward pass — it compiles only small bottleneck sub-functions with shapeless=True.

Current code state (branch: phase-o-backend-specific-models): WIP commit wip(O.7) contains broken experiments. Chunked KV (kKvChunk=64) with full-model mx::compile gives 7.5 tok/s and garbled output after chunk boundary. WORSE than 12.2 tok/s baseline.

Correct approach (from mlx-lm reference):
1. Remove full-model mx::compile from build_decode_fn() — store raw fn
2. Replace GatedDeltaNet custom Metal kernel with pure MLX ops (compilable)
3. Wrap SSM step as separate compiled fn with shapeless=True (fixed shapes in decode)
4. Revert KV to growing-via-concat, slice to actual length before SDPA
5. Keep pipelining already in moe_generate_pipelined()

mlx-lm pure-ops SSM step (no custom kernel):
  state = state * decay
  kv_mem = (state * k[..., None, :]).sum(-1)
  delta = (v - kv_mem) * beta[..., None]
  state = state + k[..., None, :] * delta[..., None]
  y = (state * q[..., None, :]).sum(-1)

Files: compute/src/compute/model/qwen3_moe_model_mlx.cpp, qwen3_moe_model_mlx.h

title: O.9: LanguageModel interface cleanup + GenerateHelper
description:
Why: Once all model families own generate() override, prefill()/decode()/reset_cache() are internal details with no callers through the interface.

What to do:
1. Extract base-class generate loop into GenerateHelper::run() in sampler.h/.cpp
2. Each model's generate(): greedy -> own pipelined path; sampling -> GenerateHelper::run()
3. Remove prefill(), decode(), reset_cache() from LanguageModel public interface
4. Remove virtual ComputeBackend* backend() const = 0 (zero callers)
5. Update all tests and callers

Prerequisite: O.7 and O.8 must be complete first.

title: O.8: Gemma pipelining (same pattern as LlamaModel O.6.4)
description:
Why: GemmaModel uses base LanguageModel::generate() which blocks between every decode step. LlamaModel got 40->71 tok/s from GPU-side pipelining. Gemma should get a similar gain.

What to do:
1. Add generate() override to GemmaModel (dispatch greedy to pipelined path)
2. Add gemma_generate_pipelined() private method — same async_eval pattern as llama_model.cpp
3. CLI verification: ./bin/neurons load gemma-3-1b-it-qat-4bit --prompt '...' --temperature 0

Files: compute/src/compute/model/gemma_model.h, gemma_model.cpp

title: O.3.3: Fuse float32 cast inside Qwen3 MoE compiled fn
description: run_decode_step does two GPU synchronisations per token: mx::async_eval for all outputs, then mx::eval(outputs[0]). The logits are bfloat16 inside the compiled fn and cast to float32 after. Moving mx::astype(logits, float32) inside the compiled fn fuses the cast into the graph, so the outputs[0] coming out is already float32 and no extra work is needed outside. Saves one bfloat16->float32 pass per step.

title: O.7d: Add qwen3_moe model_type alias to LanguageModel::load()
description: Qwen3-30B-A3B-4bit uses model_type 'qwen3_moe' (not 'qwen3_5_moe'). LanguageModel::load() currently only dispatches 'qwen3_5_moe'. Add 'qwen3_moe' as an alias in the same dispatch block so the 30B variant loads via Qwen3MoeModelMLX on Apple Silicon and Qwen3MoeModel elsewhere.

title: O.5: Remove #if MLX_BACKEND_ENABLED block from Qwen3MoeModel
description: Now that Qwen3MoeModelMLX owns the full MLX forward pass, the escape hatch inside Qwen3MoeModel is dead code. Remove MlxDecodeState, initialize_mlx_state(), build_mlx_compile_fn() from qwen3_moe_model.h and qwen3_moe_model.cpp. Qwen3MoeModel becomes a clean ComputeBackend-only implementation with no MLX awareness. Build and run tests to verify nothing breaks.

title: F.16: Jinja chat template engine
description:
Why: Hardcoded chat templates in C++ break for fine-tunes, require code changes per model family, must be kept in sync across three callers. HuggingFace standardized on Jinja2 template strings in tokenizer_config.json.

Scope:
F.16.1 — Lightweight C++ Jinja2 subset (jinja2cpp, inja, or hand-rolled). Needs: for, if/else, variable substitution, set, filters, raise_exception.
F.16.2 — Extend TokenizerConfig to extract chat_template. Expose via LanguageModel::chat_template().
F.16.3 — Replace all three hardcoded routing blocks with single apply_chat_template(model, messages).
F.16.4 — Unit tests for renderer + integration test vs Python tokenizer.apply_chat_template() reference.

Acceptance: Any HuggingFace model with chat_template field produces correct prompts without code change.

title: O.6.5: Extract shared MLX ops into llama_mlx_ops.h
description:
Why: mlx_lin, mlx_full_attn helpers are duplicated between llama_model.cpp and qwen3_moe_model_mlx.cpp. Code health only — no perf impact.

What to do: Create compute/src/compute/model/llama_mlx_ops.h with shared anonymous-namespace helpers. Include from both files. Verify build.

title: O.6.2: Direct MLX weight loading for LlamaModel (skip Tensor double-hop)
description:
Why: LlamaModel::from_model_dir loads safetensors -> Tensor map -> mx::array (double-hop). ModelLoader::load_model_mlx() already returns pair<ModelConfig, unordered_map<string,mx::array>> directly. Load-time only, no tok/s impact.

What to do: Refactor LlamaModel::from_model_dir to call ModelLoader::load_model_mlx() directly. Mirror pattern in Qwen3MoeModelMLX::from_model_dir.

title: Phase C: ROCm backend
description: Port the MLX/CUDA compute backend to AMD GPUs via HIP/ROCm. Same end-to-end inference capability as Phase B (CUDA) but targeting AMD hardware. C.1: ROCm/HIP CMake setup + ROCmBackend stub. C.2-C.5: Port elementwise kernels, rocBLAS matmul, quantized matmul, ROCm flash-attn. C.6: Wire into LanguageModel::load() dispatch and verify output matches Apple baseline. Weight format: safetensors fp16/bf16 or AWQ (same repos as CUDA).

title: O.3.4: Replace mx::argsort with mx::topk for Qwen3 MoE expert routing
description: mlx_moe_mlp_step selects top-k experts (k=8 of 256) via mx::argsort(mx::negative(logits_1d), 0). Full sort of 256 experts to get 8 winners is unnecessary. Replace with mx::topk(logits_1d, top_k) if available in installed MLX C++ headers; keep argsort as fallback. Minor win (~5% per MoE layer) but free if the API is present.

title: Phase B: CUDA backend
description:
Why: Support NVIDIA GPU inference (Linux/Windows).

Scope:
B.1 — CMake CUDA setup, CUDABackend stub, cuBLAS init
B.2 — elementwise ops as custom .cu kernels
B.3 — matmul via cublasGemmEx (fp16/bf16)
B.4 — int4 dequantization + cuBLAS GEMM
B.5 — RMSNorm and RoPE CUDA kernels
B.6 — flash-attention v2 C++ API for SDPA
B.7 — Wire in, verify output matches Apple baseline

Weight format: safetensors fp16/bf16 or AWQ (NOT MLX-quantized files — different HF repos).

title: Phase K: Multi-Node Intelligence
description:
Why: Multiple connected nodes should cooperate rather than just being switchable alternatives.

Scope:
K.1 — Model routing: AppState model->node map, auto routing, GUI node indicator
K.2 — Parallel background tasks: auto-title, summarize, embed on idle nodes
K.3 — Speculative decoding across nodes: 2-4x latency reduction
K.4 — Cascade/confidence routing: small model first, escalate on low confidence
K.5 — Failover: buffer tokens on node drop, retry on backup node

title: Phase J: File Attach and RAG
description:
Why: Users need to attach files to conversations and ask questions about their content.

Scope:
J.1 — File context injection: paperclip icon, file picker, prepend content, truncate with warning
J.2 — Embedding model support: EmbeddingModel interface, nomic-embed-text/bge-m3, dedicated embedding node
J.3 — Vector store + RAG: sqlite-vec local store, sliding window chunking, top-k cosine search, Sources section in GUI

value: mx::compile only fuses unary/binary/ternary/broadcast ops — NOT reductions (mx::sum), matmuls (mx::matmul, mx::quantized_matmul), or compound ops (rms_norm, rope, SDPA, conv1d). Limits: max_compile_depth=11 ops deep, max_compile_arrays=24 inputs. Source: build-debug/_deps/mlx-src/mlx/compile.cpp lines 23-24, 77-79. Implication for Qwen3 MoE: compile does NOT fuse the two mx::sum reductions in the GatedDeltaNet SSM step (kv_mem and y), each is a separate Metal dispatch. Full-model compile only helps with element-wise chains between non-fusable ops. The custom Metal kernel (simd_sum) was already doing all reductions in one dispatch — replacing it with pure-MLX mx::sum is strictly worse.

MLX fast_primitives.h patch is illegal — never do this again. Claude applied a patch to build-debug/_deps/mlx-src/mlx/fast_primitives.h adding CustomKernel::output_shapes() override + output_shapes_ member + constructor param. This was wrong because: (1) build dirs are not source — changes to _deps/ are not committed, not reproducible, and diverge between build/ and build-debug/; (2) patching a fetched dependency is insane — the right fix is either upstream it, use a different API, or restructure our code to avoid the constraint. The patch was never applied to build/ so the Release build (used by make run) crashed with [Primitive::output_shapes] CustomKernel cannot infer output shapes while debug tests passed. This wasted hours.

Neurons perf baselines (2026-05-03, M2 Ultra): Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3-4bit: 71 tok/s greedy (ours) vs ~80 tok/s Python mlx-lm — within 1.1x. Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit: 12.2 tok/s (ours, no optimisations) vs 88.4 tok/s Python — 7.25x gap. Target O.7: >=50 tok/s. Theoretical ceiling ~44 tok/s single-die; pipelining may push past it.

LlamaModel O.6.4 pipelining pattern: override generate() in model class, dispatch greedy (temp<1e-6) to mlx_generate_pipelined(). Loop: prefill -> CPU argmax first token -> submit step 0 -> async_eval(prev_tok_lazy) -> for each step: submit with lazy prev token, async_eval(curr), eval(prev), emit prev, rotate -> drain last. Key: pass unevaluated mx::argmax array as input to next compiled_fn call. mx::argmax returns shape {} scalar — reshape to {1} before passing as inputs[0].

mx::compile gotchas: (1) shapeless=true: never call .shape(n) and use result in reshape inside compiled fn — use -1 for dynamic dims. (2) Pipeline+EOS: pipelined path submits step N+1 before knowing step N was EOS — one wasted GPU step, acceptable. (3) Non-greedy pipelining requires GPU-side categorical sampling. (4) CustomKernel primitive in MLX does not implement output_shapes() — mx::compile(shapeless=true) throws when a custom Metal kernel is in the graph.

mx::compile on Qwen3MoE decode fn — problem and why we want it. mx::compile(shapeless=true) on the decode function fuses element-wise ops (layer norms, activations, residual adds, scales) between the big non-fusable ops (qmatmul, SDPA, custom Metal kernel). This saves memory bandwidth on small ops and avoids re-building the MLX graph on every decode step. The problem: stock MLX CustomKernel does not implement Primitive::output_shapes(), so mx::compile(shapeless=true) throws when the custom GatedDeltaNet kernel is in the graph. Claude worked around this by patching MLX locally (illegal). The correct solutions to investigate: (1) remove compile entirely — measure the tok/s cost; (2) compile only the parts of the decode fn that don't contain the custom kernel (attn + MoE sublayers, wrapped separately); (3) upstream the output_shapes fix to MLX and vendor a specific patched commit via FetchContent; (4) replace the custom Metal kernel with pure MLX ops that ARE compile-fusable (but memory says this causes 189 MB/token regression).

Phase O architecture decision: Replace single ComputeBackend primitive abstraction with backend-specific model implementations that own their entire forward pass. LlamaModelMLX / GemmaModelMLX / Qwen3MoeModelMLX — pure mx::array, no ComputeBackend. LanguageModel::load() dispatches on (model_type x BackendType). Unlocks mx::compile for MLX models and gives CUDA/ROCm models a clean path to use cuBLAS/CUTLASS/CUDA graphs without fighting a shared interface.

Qwen3MoE tok/s baselines for O.7 comparison. Pre-O.7 (no optimisations, sequential prefill, compile hack in debug only): 12.2 tok/s on M2 Ultra. Post-O.7a (batched prefill implemented, compile hack still present in debug build only): integration test in Debug build ran 128 tokens in ~6.9s = ~18.5 tok/s — NOT a valid Release baseline, Debug is always slower. Need to measure Release build (make all) with compile removed to get a real post-O.7a baseline. Python mlx-lm reference: 88.4 tok/s. Target: >=50 tok/s.

Metal heap fragmentation in compute_tests: mx::clear_cache() returns MLX free buffers to Metal heap but Metal never merges freed blocks for different-size new allocations. RSS accumulates across test suites (~36 GB peak on M2 Ultra 192 GB). Not a leak — all mx::array ref counts reach 0. Fix for <64 GB machines: run Qwen3 test separately: ./compute_tests --gtest_filter=Qwen3MoeIntegrationTest*

mlx-lm compile strategy for hybrid SSM+attention MoE models (Qwen3.6-35B-A3B): (1) Full model forward IS wrapped in @mx.compile in generate_step. (2) GatedDeltaNet uses a CUSTOM METAL KERNEL (not pure ops) in inference: gated_delta.py uses mx.fast.metal_kernel with use_kernel=not self.training. The @mx.compile fallback (_gated_delta_step_ops) is only for training/CPU. (3) The kernel accepts output_shapes= explicitly — this maps to the CustomKernel output_shapes patch in fast_primitives.h. (4) Replacing the custom kernel with pure-MLX mx::sum creates {Hv=64, Dv=128, Dk=192}=6.3 MB intermediate arrays per SSM layer × 30 layers = 189 MB/token — HUGE regression. (5) KV cache: growing via concatenate (no fixed allocation), SDPA sees actual token count. Correct approach: keep custom Metal kernel, shapeless full-model compile, growing KV, pipelining. Source: /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.13/lib/python3.13/site-packages/mlx_lm/models/gated_delta.py
